stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Some marble façades on historic buildings in Florence have been damaged by graffiti.
  • Conservators can erase some graffiti using chemical solvents.
  • For deeply absorbed graffiti, solvents are not always feasible because they can discolor or weaken the marble.
  • Conservators can use a laser-ablation technique, which removes pigment layer by layer using focused light pulses.
  • This technique is precisely controlled and largely nondestructive.
stem: The student wants to explain an advantage of the laser-ablation technique. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: To remove graffiti from marble façades on historic buildings in Florence, chemical solvents are not always feasible.
As methods for removing graffiti from marble, chemical solvents and laser ablation differ in their level of destructiveness.
Conservators can use chemical solvents to erase graffiti or remove it layer by layer using focused light pulses.
Removing pigment layer by layer with focused light pulses is more precisely controlled than dissolving it with chemical solvents, which can discolor or weaken the marble.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. It explains an advantage of laser ablation by causally linking the technique’s precision to the avoidance of marble damage.

Choice A identifies a limitation of solvents but not an advantage of laser ablation. Choice B notes the methods differ without articulating laser ablation’s advantage. Choice C lists both techniques without comparing them.
